reviews.review_description: The app is great, I get to see most of my favourite films and other movies including prime but the main problem is overheating and consuming battery rapidly.... it’s a very big problem and it just eats through your battery percentage and your OculusGo goes boiling hot if you watch a minimum of a 2 hour movie!
Will keep ya posted.. Slow dl.
Like others I’m having a overheat problem while using!

Love the Oculus Go and all the great apps.  Prime Video works almost perfectly for me.  Unfortunately I have 2 young teenage boys so I have parental controls enabled across all devices with a 4 digit pin required to play any content rated higher than age 15.  Unfortunately, in this app, all 18 content simply displays a padlock without the option to click play and type the unlock pin code.  I’ve been through all the settings on the device and been in contact with Amazon on Twitter who seem to be struggling to understand my issue.  Currently the only workaround I can find is to login to my account via a webpage and disable parent control across all devices - then log back in again afterwards to re-enable them.  It works - but is just a little annoying.

Installed. Uninstalled. Installed. No luck. Rebooted the machine several times. I get a loading 2/1 or 1/0 screen constantly. I’ve never been able to get in. Shame since this was one of the reasons to get this headset.

I give up. I watch prime all the time on my fire TV but I cannot get it to load -Or, perhaps stop loading is more accurate –which I see is a problem with others complainants noted With identical experience. I guess I’ll have to stick to Netflix and fire TV
As the title says, Netflix and Hulu needs to catch up. Great UI and video quality.

reviews.review_title: This is why I bought an Oculus Go
reviews.review_description: I originally bought my Oculus Go to watch MY digital movies in a virtual theater...not knowing that there were only a few vendors that had apps, let alone even fewer that had virtual theaters. It’s boring watching my movies in a grey void and Hulu was the only app that had a actual theater and not some big brightly lit living room. However Hulu didn’t let me watch MY movies. NOW I just need VUDU.

reviews.review_title: CAN NOT SIT WHERE I WANT
reviews.review_description: Nice cinema, but you can NOT sit where you want, i want to be able to sit further back from the screen on big screen size option... I will not subscribe or buy a movie till this is fixed. Three Stars. AND WHY CAN I NOT WATCH MY OWN STUFF ???

To me, the Prime Video VR app is the closest thing to having your own personal IMAX theatre.  Everything worked perfectly for me, and I gotta say, watching something like The Expanse on this is a true blast!

I was so looking forward to this, especially since I was able to install it. But I’ve since discovered it doesn’t work with a Canadian Prime account? I’m really hoping it will be accessible in Canada soon.

reviews.review_title: Does not work in Europe - worthless
reviews.review_description:
I was sooo happy to see Amazon release a Prime app for the Oculus. This would mean I could finally use Prime content without streaming by means of an extra device like an phone or tablet (as intermediate to my tv). 

Unfortunately I get the message this app will not work in my region (Netherlands). This makes the whole app unusable or worhless to me. 

And, yes, the abc-layout on the keyboard is annoying.
The app is amazing. I appreciate the easy option to make a beautiful big screen with a dark background!  Just another reason I recommend people get an Amazon Prime membership.

I love the implementation this application has brought t towards the VR segment. However, I've stumbled across a few irritations I'd like to see eliminated in future updates. One of my majors announces I'd like to see eliminated is the ghastly cartoony animations you are presented with upon opening the app, in the "lobby" as it were. Please remove that, or have the option to implement a different refinement on that, or have the option for a user to remove it themselves, I don't really like seeing this when I open the application, really annoys me, as I enjoy using Amazon's services.
App doesn't work in DE. When will it be official released for europe (DE)? It looks like that we are second-class customers. :(

Having had Amazon Prime for a few years and both oculus go and Samsung gear vr headsets and used both to watch Netflix, I am delighted to be able to watch amazon prime video too now "on the big screen".The cinema is great, control easy, selection of things to watch better than using prime on the TV. Love it more than using prime any other way.

Well, the first day, the PrimeVR app was great, though it does eat battery pretty fast.  The issue is that on the second day, the app goes to open and then gets stuck on "loading" for over a half hour...
For those mentioning VUDU, all you need to do is link your Amazon library with VUDU through MoviesAnywhere and you are good to go!
There are a total of 13 VR movies in this app. What's the point?
Another crappy app real waste of time
